---
title: "AMC-AMR_Stats"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
date: "2025-12-01"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 5,
  out.width = "80%"
)

```

<br>

# AMR++

Antimicrobial Resistance Genes Analysis

```{r amrplusplus_analysis, results="hide"}
library(BiocManager)
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(microViz)
library(tidylog)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)
library(readxl)
library(dunn.test)
library(decontam)
library(tibble)
library(rstatix)
library(magrittr)
library(pairwiseAdonis)
library(paletteer)
library(speedyseq)

# Load phyloseq object
rawps_amr <- readRDS(
  gzcon(url("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/grazicioletti/AMC-Calf_Nasopharyngeal/main/data/rawps_amr.rds",
            "rb"))
)

# Filter genes that require SNP Confirmation
psfilt <- rawps_amr %>% tax_select(tax_list = "SNP", strict_matches = FALSE, deselect = TRUE)

```

```{r decontam}

library(decontam)

# Decontamination using decontam package
ps2 <- psfilt %>%
  ps_mutate(
    SampleBinary = if_else(str_detect(Trt, "Negative"), true = "Control", false = "Sample") # ignore positive 
  )

# Identify contaminants using prevalence method
sample_data(ps2)$is.neg <- sample_data(ps2)$SampleBinary == "Control"
contamdf.prev <- isContaminant(ps2, method="prevalence", neg="is.neg", threshold = 0.5)

# Create phyloseq object of only contaminants in the controls
con <- rownames(contamdf.prev[contamdf.prev$contaminant == "TRUE", ])
conps <- prune_taxa(rownames(ps2@tax_table) %in% con, ps2)
negps <- subset_samples(conps, is.neg == "TRUE")

# Extract list of contaminants
contams <- rownames(contamdf.prev[contamdf.prev$contaminant == "TRUE", ])

# Display list of contaminants
cat("List of contaminants:\n")
invisible(contams)

# Remove contaminant sequences
noncontam <- prune_taxa(!rownames(ps2@tax_table) %in% contams, ps2)

# Filter controls
controls <- ps2 %>%
  ps_filter(SampleBinary == "Control", .keep_all_taxa = TRUE)

controls <- sample_data(controls)$Project_ID 

# Remove all controls
noneg <- ps_filter(noncontam, !Project_ID %in% controls)
invisible(factor(sample_data(noneg)$Trt))

# Remove positive control
noneg <- ps_filter(noneg, Trt != "PositiveControl")
invisible(factor(sample_data(noneg)$Trt))

# Prevalence-based filtering for negonly decontam
sort(phyloseq::sample_sums(noneg))

x <- phyloseq::sample_sums(noneg)

summary(x)

(noneg <- phyloseq::subset_samples(noneg, phyloseq::sample_sums(noneg) > 250))
(noneg <- phyloseq::prune_taxa(phyloseq::taxa_sums(noneg) > 0, noneg))
noneg <- noneg %>% tax_filter(
  min_prevalence = 0.01,
  min_total_abundance = 0.0001)

invisible(sample_data(noneg))

sum(contamdf.prev$contaminant) / nrow(contamdf.prev)

table(contamdf.prev$contaminant)

sample_data(noneg)$Diet <- recode_factor(
  sample_data(noneg)$Diet,
  "Ab" = "MR+A",
  "No" = "MR"
)

sample_data(noneg)$Diet <- factor(
  sample_data(noneg)$Diet,
  levels = c("MR+A","MR")
)

sample_data(noneg)$Age_wk <- factor(
  sample_data(noneg)$Age_wk,
  levels = c("1","3","5","7","9","11", "13", "15")
)

```

``` {r qc-contam}

## ── Define longitudinal analysis window
## Early and later sampling weeks (3 and 15) showed elevated and unstable contaminant burden and introduced disproportionate structure in ordination and mixed models. These timepoints were therefore excluded from primary longitudinal analyses. 

## Week 1 and 9 were borderline but still retained because contaminant distributions overlapped with adjacent weeks and sampling representation was complete.

contam_prop <- psmelt(transform_sample_counts(ps2, function(x) x / sum(x))) %>%
  mutate(is_contam = OTU %in% contams) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Age_wk) %>%
  summarise(
    contam_fraction = sum(Abundance[is_contam], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

contam_prop$Age_wk <- factor(
  as.character(contam_prop$Age_wk),
  levels = c("1","3","5","7","9","11","13","15")
)

## Save QC summary for traceability
contam_qc_summary <- contam_prop %>%
  group_by(Age_wk) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_contam = median(contam_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_contam = mean(contam_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_contam = max(contam_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(contam_qc_summary)

ggplot(contam_prop, aes(Age_wk, contam_fraction)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "grey90", colour = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    x = "Age (weeks)",
    y = "Fraction contaminant reads"
  )

## ── Define final primary analysis window
analysis_weeks <- c("1", "5","7","9","11", "13")

excluded_timepoints <- ps_filter(
  noneg,
  !as.character(Age_wk) %in% analysis_weeks
)

noneg <- ps_filter(
  noneg,
  as.character(Age_wk) %in% analysis_weeks
)

sample_data(noneg)$Age_wk <- factor(
  as.character(sample_data(noneg)$Age_wk),
  levels = analysis_weeks
)

nsamples(noneg)
table(sample_data(noneg)$Age_wk)

cat(
  "Weeks retained for primary longitudinal analysis:",
  paste(levels(sample_data(noneg)$Age_wk), collapse = ", "),
  "\n"
)

```

## Alpha Diversity

``` {r alpha_models}

## AMR ALPHA DIVERSITY — MIXED MODEL FRAMEWORK

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(glmmTMB)
library(emmeans)

sample_data(noneg)$Diet <-
  factor(sample_data(noneg)$Diet,
         levels = c("MR","MR+A"))

# dataframe
sampdf <- sample_data(noneg) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("ID")

alpha <- estimate_richness(
  noneg,
  measures = c("Shannon","Observed")
) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ID") %>%
  left_join(sampdf, by = "ID")

alpha$Animal_ID <- factor(alpha$Animal_ID)

alpha$Diet <- recode(alpha$Diet,
                     "Ab" = "MR+A",
                     "No" = "MR")

alpha$Diet <- factor(alpha$Diet,
                     levels = c("MR","MR+A"))

library(simr)
simr::simrOptions(progress = FALSE)
simrOptions(progress = FALSE)

# ------------------ MODELS ------------------

lmm_shannon_amr <- lmer(
  Shannon ~ Diet * Age_wk + (1 | Animal_ID),
  data = alpha,
  REML = FALSE
)

lmm_observed_amr <- glmmTMB(
  Observed ~ Diet * Age_wk + (1 | Animal_ID),
  family = nbinom2,
  data = alpha
)

anova(lmm_shannon_amr)
car::Anova(lmm_observed_amr, type = "III")

# ------------------ MODEL MEANS ------------------

emm_shannon_amr <- emmeans(lmm_shannon_amr, ~ Diet | Age_wk)
emm_observed_amr <- emmeans(lmm_observed_amr, ~ Diet | Age_wk, type="response")

emm_shannon_amr_df <- as.data.frame(emm_shannon_amr)
emm_observed_amr_df <- as.data.frame(emm_observed_amr)

# Pairwise Diet contrasts at each Age_wk
contrast(emm_shannon_amr, "pairwise", by = "Age_wk", adjust = "BH")

contrast(emm_observed_amr, "pairwise", by = "Age_wk", adjust = "BH", type = "response")

## ===== POST-HOC DETECTABLE EFFECT SIZE (Shannon) =====

se_diet <- summary(
  contrast(emm_shannon_amr, "pairwise", by = "Age_wk")
)$SE[1]

# approximate detectable difference (two-sided alpha 0.05)
detectable_effect <- 2 * se_diet

se_diet

detectable_effect

## ===== Detectable fold-change for Observed richness =====

coefs <- summary(lmm_observed_amr)$coefficients$cond

se_obs <- coefs["DietMR+A","Std. Error"]

## approximate detectable log-effect
detectable_log <- 2 * se_obs

## convert to fold-change
detectable_ratio <- exp(detectable_log)

se_obs

detectable_ratio

# ----------- PLOTTING
treatment_colors <- c("MR+A"="#450c54","MR"="#24868E")

# Shannon plot
ggplot(emm_shannon_amr_df,
       aes(Age_wk, emmean, fill=Diet)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity",
           position=position_dodge(.8),
           width=.7) +
 geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = emmean - SE,
      ymax = emmean + SE
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.8),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=treatment_colors) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x="Age (weeks)",
       y="Model-estimated Shannon diversity")

# Observed plot
ggplot(emm_observed_amr_df,
       aes(Age_wk, response, fill=Diet)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity",
           position=position_dodge(.8),
           width=.7) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = response - SE,
      ymax = response + SE
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.8),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=treatment_colors) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x="Age (weeks)",
       y="Model-estimated ARG richness")

```

<br><br>

## Beta Diversity

```{r beta_diversity}
## AMR BETA DIVERSITY — Aitchison

set.seed(12345)

## compositional transform (for CLR)
pst_all <- transform_sample_counts(noneg, function(x) x / sum(x))

beta_all <- pst_all %>%
  tax_transform(trans = "clr", rank = "Class")

## Aitchison distance
psdist_all <- phyloseq::distance(beta_all, method = "euclidean")

## metadata
meta_all <- data.frame(sample_data(beta_all))

## -------------------------------
## beta dispersion
bd <- betadisper(psdist_all, meta_all$Diet)
anova(bd)
permutest(bd, permutations = 999)

## PERMANOVA (longitudinal)
adonis_amr <- adonis2(
  psdist_all ~ Diet + Age_wk + Diet:Age_wk,
  data = meta_all,
  permutations = 10000,
  strata = meta_all$Animal_ID
)

adonis_amr

## PCoA ordination
ord_base <- ape::pcoa(psdist_all)

scores_df <- as.data.frame(ord_base$vectors[,1:2])
colnames(scores_df) <- c("PC1","PC2")

scores_df$SampleID <- rownames(scores_df)
meta_all$SampleID <- rownames(meta_all)

scores_df <- merge(scores_df, meta_all, by = "SampleID")

## order age legend
scores_df$Age_wk <- factor(scores_df$Age_wk, levels = c(1, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13))


## percent variance
var_exp <- round(ord_base$values$Relative_eig[1:2] * 100, 1)

## IMPORTANT:
## use the Diet labels that actually exist in your metadata
table(scores_df$Diet)

## If your levels are No / Ab:
shape_vals <- c("MR" = 17, "MR+A" = 16)
treatment_colors <- c("MR+A" = "#450c54", "MR" = "#24868E")

age_palette <- setNames(
  scico::scico(length(levels(scores_df$Age_wk)), palette = "vikO"),
  levels(scores_df$Age_wk)
)
## FINAL PLOT
ggplot(scores_df,
       aes(PC1, PC2,
           shape = Diet,
           color = Age_wk,)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.2, alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0, show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_vals, name = "Diet") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = age_palette,
    name = "Age (weeks)",
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(
      linetype = 0,  
      shape = 16,
      size = 3))
  ) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +
  stat_ellipse(
    aes(color = Diet, group = Diet),
    level = .95,
    linewidth = .9,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = treatment_colors,
                     name = "Diet") +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PCoA1 (", var_exp[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PCoA2 (", var_exp[2], "%)")
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.45, "cm"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 10, 5, 5),
    plot.title = element_blank()
  )

```

<br><br>

## Compositional plots

Broad class and class

```{r comp_amr}

# LOAD LIBRARIES
library(readr)
library(viridisLite)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(grid)

# 1) AGGREGATE TO AMR CLASS
# start from phyloseq object
psrel   <- microbiome::transform(noneg, "compositional")
psclass <- tax_glom(psrel, taxrank = "Class")

# extract matrix (KEEP sample structure intact)
mat <- as.data.frame(otu_table(psclass))

# assign class names
class_names <- as.character(tax_table(psclass)[, "Class"])
rownames(mat) <- class_names

# collapse duplicate classes ONLY (not samples!)
mat <- rowsum(mat, group = rownames(mat))

# NOW scale
z3 <- scale(t(mat))   # transpose so scaling is per class across samples
z3 <- t(z3)           # transpose back

z3 <- as.matrix(z3)

# 2) METADATA
AMC_metadata <- read_csv(
  url("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/grazicioletti/AMC-Calf_Nasopharyngeal/main/data/AMC-metadata.csv",
            "rb"),
  col_types = cols(Project_ID = col_character())
)

AMC_metadata <- AMC_metadata %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~na_if(trimws(.), "")))

keep <- sample_names(noneg)

met <- AMC_metadata %>%
  filter(Project_ID %in% keep)

met$Treatment <- case_when(
  str_detect(met$Diet, "Ab") ~ "MR+A",
  str_detect(met$Diet, "No") ~ "MR",
  TRUE ~ met$Diet
)

met <- met[match(keep, met$Project_ID), ]
stopifnot(identical(met$Project_ID, keep))

met2 <- met %>% select(Treatment, Age_wk)

rownames(met2) <- met$Project_ID
met2$Treatment <- factor(met2$Treatment, levels = c("MR+A", "MR"))
met2$Age       <- factor(as.numeric(met2$Age_wk))
met2$Age       <- factor(met2$Age, levels = c("1", "5", "7", "9", "11", "13"))


# 3) RESISTANCE CLASS (Broadclass)
merge3 <- as.data.frame(tax_table(psclass)) %>%
  select(Class, Broadclass)

rownames(merge3) <- merge3$Class
merge3 <- merge3[rownames(z3), , drop = FALSE]
stopifnot(identical(rownames(merge3), rownames(z3)))

merge3$Mean <- rowMeans(mat)[merge3$Class]

top5_df <- merge3 %>%
  group_by(Broadclass) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

top5 <- top5_df$Class

z3_sub <- z3[top5, , drop = FALSE]
merge3_sub <- merge3[top5, , drop = FALSE]
stopifnot(identical(rownames(z3_sub), rownames(merge3_sub)))

# force resistance class order
merge3_sub$Broadclass <- factor(
  merge3_sub$Broadclass,
  levels = c("Biocides", "Drugs", "Metals", "Multi-compound")
)

# reorder rows in the matrix and metadata FIRST
row_idx <- order(merge3_sub$Broadclass)

z3_sub     <- z3_sub[row_idx, , drop = FALSE]
merge3_sub <- merge3_sub[row_idx, , drop = FALSE]

# then make row_split from the reordered factor
row_split <- merge3_sub$Broadclass

# 4) ANNOTATIONS
age_palette <- setNames(
  viridis(length(levels(met2$Age)), option = "G"),
  levels(met2$Age)
)

ha_col <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  df = met2[, c("Treatment", "Age")],
  col = list(
    Treatment = treatment_colors,
    Age       = age_palette
  )
)

resistance_colors <- c(
  "Biocides"       = "#552F7A",
  "Drugs"          = "#7C5F98",
  "Metals"         = "#B09FC1",
  "Multi-compound" = "#CABED6"
)

ha_row <- rowAnnotation(
  Resistance = merge3_sub$Broadclass,
  col = list(Resistance = resistance_colors),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  annotation_name_gp = gpar(fontsize = 0)
)

# 5) ORDER COLUMNS 
column_split <- data.frame(
  Treatment = met2$Treatment,
  Age = met2$Age
)

# CLEAN TAX PREFIXES
rownames(z3_sub) <- 
  gsub("_", " ", rownames(z3_sub),
                         ignore.case = TRUE)

rownames(z3_sub) <- 
  gsub("[a-z]__", "", rownames(z3_sub),
                         ignore.case = TRUE)

rownames(z3_sub) <- gsub("betalactams", "β-lactams",
                         rownames(z3_sub),
                         ignore.case = TRUE)

col_split <- factor(
  paste(met2$Treatment,
        met2$Age,
        sep = "_"),
  levels = c(
    "MR_5","MR_7","MR_9","MR_11",
    "MR+A_5","MR+A_7","MR+A_9","MR+A_11"
  )
)

# DEFINE COLORS AGAIN
treatment_colors <- c(
  "MR+A" = "#450c54",
  "MR"   = "#24868E"
)

# CLEAN FACTOR
met2$Treatment <- factor(met2$Treatment, levels = c("MR+A", "MR"))

# RECREATE ANNOTATION 
ha_col <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  df = met2[, c("Treatment", "Age")],
  col = list(
    Treatment = treatment_colors,
    Age       = age_palette
  )
)

# 4️⃣ COLUMN SPLIT
col_split <- factor(
  paste(met2$Treatment, met2$Age, sep = "_"),
  levels = c(
    "MR_5","MR_7","MR_9","MR_11",
    "MR+A_5","MR+A_7","MR+A_9","MR+A_11"
  )
)

# 5️⃣ BUILD HEATMAP
ht <- Heatmap(
  z3_sub,
  name            = "Z-score",
  top_annotation  = ha_col,
  left_annotation = ha_row,

  row_split       = row_split,
  column_split    = col_split,

  row_title       = NULL,
  column_title    = NULL,

  row_gap         = unit(0.5, "mm"),
  column_gap      = unit(0, "mm"),

  cluster_rows        = TRUE,
  cluster_row_slices  = FALSE,
  row_dend_reorder    = FALSE,

  cluster_columns        = TRUE,
  cluster_column_slices  = FALSE,

  show_column_names = FALSE,

  col = colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), viridis(3, option = "G")),
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
  border = FALSE,
  heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Z-score")
)

draw(ht)

# ── AFTER draw(ht), compute z-means ───────────────────────────────────────────

# STEP 1: fresh met2 (character, not factor)
met2_plain <- data.frame(
  Treatment = trimws(met$Treatment),
  Age_wk    = met$Age_wk,
  row.names = met$Project_ID,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# STEP 2: rebuild z3_sub with ORIGINAL rownames (before gsub)
z3_sub_raw <- z3[top5, , drop = FALSE]   # top5 uses original class names

# STEP 3: align
common <- intersect(colnames(z3_sub_raw), rownames(met2_plain))
cat("Shared samples:", length(common), "\n")

z3_sub_raw <- z3_sub_raw[, common, drop = FALSE]
met_use    <- met2_plain[common, , drop = FALSE]
met_use    <- met_use[colnames(z3_sub_raw), , drop = FALSE]

cat("MR:", sum(met_use$Treatment == "MR"),
    "| MR+A:", sum(met_use$Treatment == "MR+A"), "\n")

# STEP 4: group means
group_MR  <- which(met_use$Treatment == "MR")
group_MRA <- which(met_use$Treatment == "MR+A")
stopifnot(length(group_MR) > 0, length(group_MRA) > 0)

z_mean_MR  <- rowMeans(z3_sub_raw[, group_MR,  drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
z_mean_MRA <- rowMeans(z3_sub_raw[, group_MRA, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)

# STEP 5: clean names for display (same gsub as heatmap)
display_names <- rownames(z3_sub_raw)
display_names <- gsub("_", " ", display_names)
display_names <- gsub("[a-z]__", "", display_names, ignore.case = TRUE)
display_names <- gsub("betalactams", "β-lactams", display_names, ignore.case = TRUE)

# STEP 6: build table in descending overall order (matches heatmap top→bottom)
z_mean_by_trt <- data.frame(
  Class = display_names,
  MR    = z_mean_MR,
  MR_A  = z_mean_MRA
) %>%
  mutate(overall = (MR + MR_A) / 2) %>%
  arrange(desc(overall))

print(z_mean_by_trt)

```


``` {r reproducibility }
# SAVE AMR REPRODUCIBLE OUTPUT
dir.create("amr", showWarnings = FALSE)

# 1. Decontamination
write.csv(contamdf.prev,
          "amr/decontam_results.csv")

writeLines(contams,
           "amr/list_of_contaminants.txt")

# 2. Alpha diversity
write.csv(alpha,
          "amr/alpha_raw.csv")

# (optional but recommended)
# write.csv(alpha_clean, "amr/alpha_clean.csv")

# 3. Mixed models
saveRDS(lmm_shannon_amr,
        "amr/lmm_shannon_model.rds")

saveRDS(lmm_observed_amr,
        "amr/lmm_observed_model.rds")

capture.output(summary(lmm_shannon_amr),
               file="amr/lmm_shannon_summary.txt")

capture.output(summary(lmm_observed_amr),
               file="amr/lmm_observed_summary.txt")

# 4. EMMs
write.csv(emm_shannon_amr_df,
          "amr/emmeans_shannon.csv")

write.csv(emm_observed_amr_df,
          "amr/emmeans_observed.csv")

# 5. Beta diversity
saveRDS(psdist_all,
        "amr/distance_matrix_amr.rds")

write.csv(meta_all,
          "amr/beta_metadata_amr.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(as.data.frame(adonis_amr),
          "amr/permanova_amr.csv")

write.csv(scores_df,
          "amr/pcoa_scores_amr.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(
  data.frame(Variance = ord_base$values$Relative_eig),
  "amr/pcoa_variance_amr.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 6. Final phyloseq
saveRDS(noneg,
        "amr/phyloseq_amr_filtered.rds")

# 7. Power analysis
write.csv(
  data.frame(
    metric = c("Shannon", "Observed"),
    se_diet = c(se_diet, se_obs),
    detectable_difference = c(detectable_effect, NA),
    detectable_ratio = c(NA, detectable_ratio)
  ),
  "amr/posthoc_sensitivity_summary.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("✓ AMR reproducible outputs saved\n")

```
